arguments.task: Open and read official Brex pages that establish: (1) what Brex API is and its developer docs overview, (2) authentication methods documented for developers, (3) API reference style to determine REST vs GraphQL, (4) pricing or access-tier language for APIs or developer platform if available, (5) any official mention of MCP server or model context protocol. Prefer canonical developer.brex.com or first-party brex.com pages. Return the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted findings with quotations or close paraphrases from page text. Do not use search-result snippets. If docs are JS-rendered, wait for content to load.

Use Google only to find official Brex pages, then open the strongest official pages on developer.brex.com or brex.com about API auth, API reference, pricing/access, and MCP if any. Read the page contents themselves and return canonical URLs plus extracted findings from those official pages only. Do not rely on search-result snippets for conclusions.

input.thought: Launch two independent browser runs to gather official Brex documentation and canonical evidence pages.

arguments.task: Read the official Brex Developer authentication page carefully. Return only findings supported by the page text: exact auth method(s) documented there, how credentials are created, how they are sent in requests, and any expiry or scope notes. Include the canonical page URL and concise quotes or close paraphrases from the page text.

Read the official Brex Partner authentication page carefully. Return only findings supported by the page text: exact auth method(s) documented there, OAuth/OpenID details, grant types, and any token endpoint or scope notes. Include the canonical page URL and concise quotes or close paraphrases from the page text.

Read the official Brex MCP page carefully. Return only findings supported by the page text: whether Brex has an MCP server, its URL, auth methods specifically documented for MCP, and any beta/gating requirements. Include the canonical page URL and concise quotes or close paraphrases from the page text.

Read the official Brex pricing page carefully. Return only findings supported by the page text: whether Brex API access is included in a self-serve plan, the named plan and price, and any relevant gating language. Include the canonical page URL and concise quotes or close paraphrases from the page text.

raw_result.description: Brex provides a developer platform for programmatic access to business finance workflows such as expenses, payments, transactions, budgets, travel, and webhooks.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Brex developer docs show developer authentication with user-generated bearer tokens from the Brex Dashboard on the Developer authentication page, partner authentication with OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect on the Partner authentication page, and the Brex MCP page documents OAuth and API keys for MCP access.
raw_result.gating_reason: Brex pricing states that 'Brex API access' is included in the Essentials plan at $0/user/month, indicating self-serve free access at least for baseline API availability.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs expose multiple APIs including Accounting, Budgets, Expenses, Fields, Onboarding, Payments, Team, Transactions, Travel, and Webhooks; the docs present a broad REST API surface for Brex financial operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Brex has an official MCP offering documented at developer.brex.com/docs/mcp with server URL https://api.brex.com/mcp. The page describes it as beta and notes prerequisites such as accepting the Developer API agreement and enabling the 'Brex in AI assistants' beta feature.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No specific public rate-limit details were verified from the gathered page text.
raw_result.notable_signals: Brex publishes dedicated developer authentication and partner OAuth documentation, a broad API catalog, and an official MCP server page; MCP is explicitly marked beta, while standard API access appears included even on the free Essentials plan.

result.description: Brex provides a developer platform for programmatic access to business finance workflows such as expenses, payments, transactions, budgets, travel, and webhooks.

result.gating_reason: Brex pricing states that 'Brex API access' is included in the Essentials plan at $0/user/month, indicating self-serve free access at least for baseline API availability.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs expose multiple APIs including Accounting, Budgets, Expenses, Fields, Onboarding, Payments, Team, Transactions, Travel, and Webhooks; the docs present a broad REST API surface for Brex financial operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Brex has an official MCP offering documented at developer.brex.com/docs/mcp with server URL https://api.brex.com/mcp. The page describes it as beta and notes prerequisites such as accepting the Developer API agreement and enabling the 'Brex in AI assistants' beta feature.

result.rate_limit_notes: No specific public rate-limit details were verified from the gathered page text.
result.notable_signals: Brex publishes dedicated developer authentication and partner OAuth documentation, a broad API catalog, and an official MCP server page; MCP is explicitly marked beta, while standard API access appears included even on the free Essentials plan.
